title: Side Table with Wireless Charger
summary: This sturdy side table with a built in wireless charger is the perfect addition to your living area.
description: This sturdy side table with a built in wireless charger is the perfect addition to your living area. The rounded edges give this table a modern look. The best part is: You can customize it to fit your needs!

materials.name: Box of 1 1/4" Kreg Pocket Hole Screws (see tools list)

steps.title: Step One
steps.body: Cut the top boards about 2” longer than you need. Set your Pocket hole jig to 3/4” and the stop collar on the drill bit to 3/4”. Drill pocket holes on the backside of the boards, then glue and join them together using 1 1/4” pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Step Two
steps.body: Cut the top to size with the Kreg Jig Track saw.

steps.title: Step Three
steps.body: Cut the legs about 2” longer than you need. Pocket hole and glue them together like you did the top.

steps.title: Step Four
steps.body: Cut the legs to size with the Kreg Jig Track saw.

steps.title: Step Five
steps.body: Using a template such as a bowl or a cup, trace it on both sides of the top. This will be the template for your oval shape. Cut out the shape with a jigsaw.

steps.title: Step Six
steps.body: Repeat this same process on the legs.

steps.title: Step Seven
steps.body: Optional step: Use 1/4” roundover bit to roundover all sides of the top and legs.

steps.title: Step Eight
steps.body: Trace the shape of the wireless charger on the underside of the table top. Mark 1/2” depth on the forstner bit using tape. Drill in the center until you reach the tape.

steps.title: Step Nine
steps.body: With your router and upcut spiral bit, make shallow passes until you reach the same depth as the bottom of the hole you made with the forstner bit.

steps.title: step Ten
steps.body: Using double sided tape, push your wireless charger into the hole you made. Make sure to test to see if your phone charges.

steps.title: Step Eleven
steps.body: Attach the top support between the two oval shapes you made for the legs using pocket holes and screws.

steps.title: Step Twelve
steps.body: Attach the middle support about 10” up from the bottom of the legs using pocket holes and screws.

steps.title: Step Thirteen
steps.body: Sand, stain and you have a brand new table!
